title: ctenitis warburii
intro: Ctenitis warburii, also known as Warburton's fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to New Zealand and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and alleviating pain, as well as having antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of Ctenitis warburii are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which is then used to treat various ailments.
